skill.name: caveman
skill.description: Ultra-compressed communication mode. Cuts token usage ~75% by dropping filler, articles, and pleasantries while keeping full technical accuracy.
skill.summary: Ultra-compressed communication mode. Cuts token usage ~75% by dropping filler while keeping technical accuracy.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged markdown backticks (code formatting) as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' and YAML frontmatter 'description' as 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. All findings are false positives. This is a pure text-processing skill containing only documentation and communication guidelines. No code execution, network access, or file operations exist.

high_findings.title: False Positive: YAML Frontmatter Description Flagged
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'description:' in YAML frontmatter (line 3) as 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. This is documentation metadata, not cryptographic code. FALSE POSITIVE.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: YAML frontmatter 'description:' is standard documentation syntax, not cryptographic algorithm detection

high_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Command Execution
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged backtick characters in markdown as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. These are markdown code formatting delimiters (inline code and code blocks), not shell command invocations. FALSE POSITIVE.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Backticks are markdown syntax for code formatting. No Ruby/shell command execution is present in this documentation-only file

content.user_title: Cut token usage by 75% with ultra-brief responses
content.value_statement: Verbose AI responses consume excessive tokens and slow workflows. This skill compresses communication to essential content only while preserving technical accuracy, enabling faster and cheaper interactions with Claude, Codex, or Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Drops articles, filler words, and pleasantries from responses
Uses fragments and abbreviated technical terms when appropriate
Maintains full technical accuracy while reducing verbosity
Persists mode across conversation turns until explicitly disabled
Applies arrow notation (X -> Y) for causality
Auto-exits for security warnings and irreversible actions
limitations: May reduce readability for complex multi-step explanations
Not suitable for communicating with non-technical users
Auto-clarity exceptions may cause inconsistent compression
Cannot compress code or error messages further

use_cases.title: Senior developers in cost-sensitive projects
use_cases.description: Reduces token usage dramatically for teams working with usage limits or budget constraints, keeping all technical details intact.

use_cases.title: Rapid prototyping and iteration
use_cases.description: Speeds up feedback loops during active development by eliminating conversational overhead between code changes.

use_cases.title: High-frequency automation scripts
use_cases.description: Makes automated Claude Codex interactions more efficient for CI/CD pipelines and tooling scripts.

prompt_templates.title: Activate caveman mode
prompt_templates.prompt: Use caveman mode for our session
prompt_templates.scenario: Single activation enables compressed responses for the entire conversation

prompt_templates.title: Quick technical question
prompt_templates.prompt: Why is my React component re-rendering unnecessarily?

prompt_templates.title: Request code fix
prompt_templates.prompt: Fix the authentication token expiry check

prompt_templates.title: Toggle off when needed
prompt_templates.prompt: Stop caveman mode and explain this database concept in detail
prompt_templates.scenario: Returns to normal verbose mode for complex topics

output_examples.input: Why is my React component re-rendering unnecessarily?
output_examples.output: Inline obj prop -> new ref -> re-render. Use useMemo.
Pool = reuse DB conn. Skip handshake -> fast under load.
Bug in auth middleware. Token expiry check use < not <=. Fix:

best_practices: Activate once at session start rather than repeatedly requesting brevity
Use normal mode for complex multi-step explanations requiring clarity
Say 'stop caveman' to exit cleanly when finished
anti_patterns: Do not use caveman mode for user-facing documentation or commit messages
Do not switch modes mid-explanation as it creates inconsistent responses
Do not assume technical audiences will tolerate fragments for simple concepts

faq.question: Does caveman mode make mistakes to save tokens?
faq.answer: No. Technical accuracy remains complete. Only filler and pleasantries are removed.

faq.question: How do I exit caveman mode?
faq.answer: Say 'stop caveman' or 'normal mode'. The mode persists until explicitly disabled.

faq.question: Does it work with all Claude models?
faq.answer: Yes. Caveman mode works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code by modifying response style.

faq.question: What happens during security warnings?
faq.answer: The skill auto-activates normal mode temporarily for security warnings and irreversible actions.

faq.question: Can I use caveman mode for code generation?
faq.answer: Yes. Code blocks remain unchanged. Only the surrounding explanation is compressed.

faq.question: How much token savings should I expect?
faq.answer: Approximately 75% reduction in tokens for conversational text while keeping full technical substance.
